incidents.vector: Exploited known vulnerabilities in end-of-life Mikrotik and TP-Link SOHO routers to hijack DNS settings and intercept OAuth authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users.
incidents.impact: State-backed hackers silently harvested authentication tokens from users across 18,000+ networks targeting government agencies, law enforcement, and critical infrastructure. FBI conducted court-authorized 'Operation Masquerade' to remediate compromised U.S. routers.

incidents.vector: Phishing attack targeting an attorney to gain unauthorized access to a limited number of client files.
incidents.impact: The Silent Ransom Group posted stolen client materials online. Files for 10 clients were accessed, including work related to the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit involving intellectual property matters.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-35616, an improper access control zero-day in Fortinet FortiClient EMS allowing unauthenticated remote code execution.
incidents.impact: CISA added the vulnerability to the KEV catalog. Widespread exploitation observed in the wild beginning March 31, timed to Easter weekend when security teams operate at reduced capacity. Second FortiClient EMS vulnerability in three weeks.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, education, and finance organizations (US, UK, Australia)
incidents.vector: Medusa ransomware group exploiting zero-day and N-day vulnerabilities in web-facing systems, moving from initial access to data exfiltration and ransomware deployment within 24 hours.
incidents.impact: Multiple organizations compromised including the University of Mississippi Medical Center and Passaic County, New Jersey. Microsoft warns of the group's increasing speed and exploitation of vulnerabilities before public disclosure.

incidents.vector: Exploited a compromised version of the Trivy security scanner (via TeamPCP supply chain attack) to steal an AWS API key, then exfiltrated 92 GB of data from the Commission's Europa.eu platform.
incidents.impact: Data from 42 internal clients and at least 29 EU entities potentially stolen, including ~52,000 files related to outbound email communications. Stolen data surfaced on ShinyHunters' dark web site on March 28.

incidents.victim: Akira ransomware victims (manufacturing, education, healthcare, finance)
incidents.vector: Exploitation of zero-day vulnerabilities, purchasing exploits from initial access brokers, and targeting VPNs lacking MFA, achieving encryption in as little as one hour from initial access.
incidents.impact: At least $245 million in ransom payments collected. FBI and CISA previously flagged Akira as a top ransomware group. The group targets SMBs with a double-extortion model and intermittent encryption for speed.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized network access identified on March 28; specific initial access method not disclosed.
incidents.impact: The toymaker took systems offline and activated its business continuity plan. Order processing and shipping disrupted for several weeks. Shares fell 3% on the disclosure.

incidents.victim: Amazon Web Services Bahrain data center
incidents.vector: Kinetic military strike on Amazon's cloud computing facility in Bahrain as part of broader Iranian targeting of Gulf civilian and industrial infrastructure.
incidents.impact: AWS Bahrain region taken offline. IRGC-affiliated media announced further waves of strikes targeting steel industries and major bridges across Gulf states.

incidents.vector: Italian surveillance company ASIGINT (subsidiary of SIO) distributed a fake WhatsApp app rigged with spyware to approximately 200 targeted users.
incidents.impact: Highly targeted surveillance campaign. Second time in 15 months Meta has disrupted spyware activity in Italy, following the Paragon spyware exposure in early 2025.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-5281, a use-after-free vulnerability in Dawn (WebGPU implementation) — the fourth Chrome zero-day exploited in attacks in 2026.
incidents.impact: CISA added the vulnerability to the KEV catalog with an urgent patch deadline. All Chrome users on affected versions were vulnerable to remote code execution.

incidents.victim: Axios JavaScript library users (100M+ weekly downloads)
incidents.vector: Social-engineered the lead Axios npm maintainer via a fake company video call to install malware, then hijacked the npm account to publish trojanized versions (1.14.1 and 0.30.4) containing a cross-platform RAT.
incidents.impact: An estimated 600,000 downloads of the compromised versions before detection. The malware harvested cloud credentials, SSH keys, and API tokens across Windows, macOS, and Linux. Described as one of the most impactful npm supply chain attacks on record.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-3055, a critical out-of-bounds read vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler allowing unauthenticated attackers to leak sensitive memory — resembling previous CitrixBleed attacks.
incidents.impact: CISA ordered federal agencies to patch immediately. The vulnerability echoes CitrixBleed (2023) and CitrixBleed Two (2025), both of which were used by ransomware gangs and nation-state hackers against hospitals and critical infrastructure.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2025-53521, an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager, upgraded from DoS to critical RCE.
incidents.impact: F5 confirmed active exploitation. NCSC UK urged organizations to isolate affected systems and investigate for compromise. Commonly deployed in large enterprise environments.

incidents.victim: 300+ organizations across critical infrastructure (US, UK, Israel, UAE, Europe, Saudi Arabia)
incidents.vector: Password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments. Targeting correlated with cities struck by Iranian missiles, suggesting intelligence support for kinetic operations.
incidents.impact: Over 300 organizations hit across critical infrastructure sectors. Activity overlaps with known Iranian state-sponsored groups. The campaign appears designed to support bombing damage assessment and kinetic military operations.

incidents.victim: Aqua Security Trivy, Checkmarx KICS, BerriAI LiteLLM, and 47+ npm packages
incidents.vector: TeamPCP exploited incomplete credential rotation in open-source projects' GitHub accounts to inject infostealers and a self-replicating worm (CanisterWorm) into trusted software supply chains across multiple waves.
incidents.impact: Estimated 500,000+ machines infected with 300+ GB of exfiltrated data including cloud tokens, SSH keys, and Kubernetes secrets. At least 16 victim organizations published on leak sites. The group also deployed a wiper targeting systems in Iran's timezone.

incidents.vector: Wiper malware deployed by the Iranian government-connected Handala group in retaliation over the US-Iran conflict.
incidents.impact: The wiper attack damaged Stryker's order processing, manufacturing, and shipping systems. The company reported being fully operational again by early April after three weeks of disruption, with support from cyber experts and government agencies.

incidents.vector: Exploited CVE-2026-3502 in TrueConf video conferencing software's updater to distribute malicious updates via a compromised government-operated on-premises server, deploying Havoc and ShadowPad malware.
incidents.impact: Dozens of government entities across an unnamed Southeast Asian country were served weaponized updates through their own IT department's TrueConf server. CISA gave U.S. federal agencies two weeks to patch.

incidents.vector: German Federal Criminal Police (BKA) identified and publicly named 31-year-old Russian Daniil Shchukin as the operator of GandCrab and REvil ransomware gangs, along with co-conspirator Anatoly Kravchuk.
incidents.impact: The two operators are linked to at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion causing €35 million in economic damage in Germany alone. GandCrab/REvil collectively extorted over $2 billion from victims worldwide. Shchukin is believed to reside in Krasnodar, Russia.

incidents.victim: Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad botnet victims (incl. DoD)
incidents.vector: Operators compromised 3+ million IoT devices (routers, web cameras) to build four botnets capable of record-breaking DDoS attacks, frequently demanding extortion payments.
incidents.impact: DOJ, Canada, and Germany dismantled the botnet infrastructure. The botnets launched hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks including against U.S. Department of Defense networks.

incidents.victim: NCSC UK warns messaging app users
incidents.vector: Russia-based actors targeting high-risk individuals via WhatsApp, Signal, and Messenger by tricking victims into sharing login codes, adding attacker devices to accounts, or clicking malicious QR codes.
incidents.impact: NCSC issued advisory alongside international partners warning of growing malicious activity targeting government officials' and high-profile individuals' messaging accounts. Follows prior FSB and IRGC targeting campaigns.

incidents.vector: Automated credential harvesting campaign exploiting React2Shell RCE vulnerability in Next.js applications using a custom 'NEXUS Listener' framework to extract cloud tokens, database credentials, and SSH keys from hundreds of hosts.
incidents.impact: The campaign uses high-speed automation to rapidly harvest high-value credentials and establish persistent access. Centralized aggregation of stolen data enables attackers to map infrastructure for follow-on attacks.

incidents.vector: Pay2Key ransomware deployed using compromised administrative credentials and living-off-the-land techniques against a U.S. healthcare target.
incidents.impact: Assessed by analysts as state-aligned disruption rather than financially motivated — indicating deliberate targeting of U.S. healthcare infrastructure during the Iran conflict rather than opportunistic cybercrime.
